cases.name: returning_test-1
cases.sql: INSERT INTO users (name, email) VALUES ('Alice', 'alice@example.com') RETURNING *

cases.name: returning_test-2
cases.sql: INSERT INTO users (name, email) VALUES ('Bob', 'bob@example.com') RETURNING id, name

cases.name: returning_test-3
cases.sql:
INSERT INTO users (name, email) VALUES 
         ('Charlie', 'charlie@example.com'),
         ('David', 'david@example.com'),
         ('Eve', 'eve@example.com')

cases.name: returning_test-4
cases.sql: UPDATE users SET email = name || '@updated.com' WHERE name LIKE '%e' RETURNING name, email

cases.name: returning_test-5
cases.sql: DELETE FROM users WHERE name = 'NonExistent' RETURNING *

            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            name TEXT,
            value INTEGER,
            description TEXT

cases.name: batch_insert_comprehensive_test-11
cases.sql:
CREATE TABLE returning_test (
            id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
            name TEXT,
            created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
        )

cases.sql: INSERT INTO returning_test (name) VALUES ('first') RETURNING id, name

cases.sql: INSERT INTO returning_test (name) VALUES ('second'), ('third')

cases.name: batch_insert_comprehensive_test-14
cases.sql: SELECT id, name FROM returning_test ORDER BY id

cases.name: batch_insert_comprehensive_test-15
cases.sql:
CREATE TABLE constraint_test (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            email TEXT UNIQUE,
            age INTEGER CHECK (age >= 0),
            name VARCHAR(10)
        )

cases.name: batch_insert_comprehensive_test-16
cases.sql: INSERT INTO constraint_test (id, email, age, name) VALUES (1, 'test@example.com', 25, 'Test')

cases.name: batch_insert_comprehensive_test-17
cases.sql:
INSERT INTO constraint_test (id, email, age, name) VALUES
            (2, 'new@example.com', 30, 'New'),
            (3, 'test@example.com', 35, 'Duplicate')

cases.name: batch_insert_comprehensive_test-18
cases.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM constraint_test

cases.name: batch_insert_comprehensive_test-19
cases.sql:
INSERT INTO constraint_test (id, email, age, name) VALUES
            (4, 'valid@example.com', 40, 'Valid'),
            (5, 'invalid@example.com', -5, 'Invalid')

cases.sql:
INSERT INTO constraint_test (id, email, age, name) VALUES
            (6, 'long@example.com', 30, 'VeryLongNameThatExceedsLimit')

cases.name: batch_insert_datetime_functions_test-1
cases.sql:
CREATE TABLE datetime_test (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            name TEXT,
            created_date DATE,
            created_time TIME,
            created_timestamp TIMESTAMP
        )

cases.name: batch_insert_datetime_functions_test-2
cases.sql:
INSERT INTO datetime_test (id, name, created_date, created_time, created_timestamp) VALUES 
            (1, 'test1', CURRENT_DATE, CURRENT_TIME, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP),
            (2, 'test2', CURRENT_DATE, CURRENT_TIME, NOW()),
            (3, 'test3', '2025-01-01', '14:30:00', '2025-01-01 14:30:00')

cases.name: batch_insert_datetime_functions_test-3
cases.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM datetime_test

cases.name: batch_insert_datetime_functions_test-4
cases.sql: SELECT name, created_date, created_time, created_timestamp FROM datetime_test WHERE id = 3

cases.sql:
INSERT INTO mixed_datetime_test (id, description, event_date, event_timestamp) VALUES 
            (1, 'Current', CURRENT_DATE, NOW()),

            (4, 'Functions', CURRENT_DATE, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)

cases.name: batch_insert_datetime_functions_test-10
cases.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM mixed_datetime_test

cases.name: batch_insert_error_test-1
cases.sql:
CREATE TABLE test_table (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            name TEXT,
            value INTEGER
        )

cases.name: batch_insert_error_test-2
cases.sql:
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, value) VALUES 
            (1, 'test1', 100),
            (2, 'test2'),  -- Missing value column
            (3, 'test3', 300)

cases.name: batch_insert_error_test-3
cases.sql:
CREATE TABLE date_test (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            event_date DATE
        )

cases.name: batch_insert_error_test-4
cases.sql:
INSERT INTO date_test (id, event_date) VALUES 
            (1, '2025-01-01'),
            (2, '01/15/2025'),  -- Wrong format
            (3, '2025-01-03')

cases.name: batch_insert_error_test-5
cases.sql:
CREATE TABLE time_test (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            event_time TIME
        )

cases.name: batch_insert_error_test-6
cases.sql:
INSERT INTO time_test (id, event_time) VALUES 
            (1, '14:30:00'),
            (2, '2:30 PM'),  -- Wrong format
            (3, '16:45:00')

cases.name: batch_insert_error_test-7
cases.sql:
CREATE TABLE timestamp_test (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            event_timestamp TIMESTAMP
        )

cases.name: batch_insert_error_test-8
cases.sql:
INSERT INTO timestamp_test (id, event_timestamp) VALUES 
            (1, '2025-01-01 14:30:00'),
            (2, '2025-01-02T15:45:00Z'),  -- ISO format not supported
            (3, '2025-01-03 16:00:00')

cases.name: batch_insert_error_test-9
cases.sql:
CREATE TABLE unique_test (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            email TEXT UNIQUE
        )

cases.name: batch_insert_error_test-10
cases.sql: INSERT INTO unique_test (id, email) VALUES (1, 'existing@example.com')

cases.name: batch_insert_error_test-11
cases.sql:
INSERT INTO unique_test (id, email) VALUES 
            (2, 'new1@example.com'),
            (3, 'existing@example.com'),  -- Duplicate
            (4, 'new2@example.com')

cases.name: batch_insert_error_test-12
cases.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM unique_test

cases.name: multirow_insert_returning_test-1
cases.sql:
INSERT INTO users (name, email) VALUES 
         ('Alice', 'alice@example.com'),
         ('Bob', 'bob@example.com'),
         ('Charlie', 'charlie@example.com')
         RETURNING id, name

cases.name: multirow_insert_returning_test-2
cases.sql: CREATE TABLE temp_users (name TEXT, email TEXT)

cases.name: multirow_insert_returning_test-3
cases.sql: INSERT INTO temp_users VALUES ('David', 'david@example.com'), ('Eve', 'eve@example.com')

cases.name: multirow_insert_returning_test-4
cases.sql:
INSERT INTO users (name, email) 
         SELECT name, email FROM temp_users
         RETURNING id, name, email

cases.name: multirow_insert_returning_test-5
cases.sql: SELECT * FROM users ORDER BY id

cases.name: multirow_insert_returning_test-6
cases.sql:
INSERT INTO categories (name, description, created_at) 
         SELECT p0::VARCHAR, p1::TEXT, p2::TIMESTAMP WITHOUT TIME ZONE 
         FROM (VALUES 
           ('Technology', 'Posts about technology and programming', '2025-01-25 12:00:00'::timestamp, 0), 
           ('Lifestyle', 'Posts about lifestyle and personal development', '2025-01-25 12:00:01'::timestamp, 1)
         ) AS imp_sen(p0, p1, p2, sen_counter) 
         ORDER BY sen_counter 
         RETURNING categories.id, categories.id AS id__1

cases.name: multirow_insert_returning_test-7
cases.sql: SELECT * FROM categories ORDER BY id
